title: Authors and Citation
text: Jaron Arbet. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jaron Arbet (2026). statplot: Convenient Tools Generating Standard Statistical Plots. R package version 0.2.0, https://statgencore.github.io/statplot/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {statplot: Convenient Tools for Generating Standard Statistical Plots},   author = {{Jaron Arbet}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://statgencore.github.io/statplot/}, }

what: Convenient Tools for Generating Standard Statistical Plots
title: Convenient Tools for Generating Standard Statistical Plots
text: statplot R package designed simplify creation figures used common statistical analyses. provides convenient wrapper functions generating clean, publication-ready visualizations minimal code.

title: Convenient Tools for Generating Standard Statistical Plots
text: can install development version statplot GitHub :
code: pak::pak("StatGenCore/statplot")

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: Combines numeric vector p-values single p-value using variety methods.  Note CMC MCM recommended Chen 2022.

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
code: combine_pvalues(p)

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: p Numeric vector p-values.

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: named vector combined p-values method

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: Chen, Z. Robust tests combining p-values arbitrary dependency structures. Sci Rep 12, 3158 (2022). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-022-07094-7

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues

what: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma
title: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma
text: lightweight helper formats numeric values commas disables scientific notation.

title: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma
code: comma(x)

title: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma
text: x Numeric vector format.

title: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma
text: Character vector numbers formatted using , thousands separator.

title: Format numbers with comma as thousands separator — comma

what: Example tidy expression dataset for heatmap plotting — ex_data_heatmap
title: Example tidy expression dataset for heatmap plotting — ex_data_heatmap
text: small long-format data frame simulated gene expression values across three samples, intended use plot_heatmap examples tests.

title: Example tidy expression dataset for heatmap plotting — ex_data_heatmap
code: ex_data_heatmap

title: Example tidy expression dataset for heatmap plotting — ex_data_heatmap
text: data frame 9 rows 10 columns: external_gene_name Character. Gene identifier (GeneA–GeneC). sample Character. Sample identifier (S1–S3). expression Numeric. Simulated expression value. is_immune_gene Character. Whether gene immune gene ("yes" ""). direction Character. Expression direction ("" ""). log2fc Numeric. Log2 fold change. sample_type Character. Sample type ("input" "IP"). condition Character. Experimental condition ("healthy" "EAE"). group Character. Sample grouping ("G1" "G2"). qc_score Numeric. Quality control score (0–1).

title: Example tidy expression dataset for heatmap plotting — ex_data_heatmap
text: Generated inst/prepare_data/data_exprs_small.R.

title: Format p-values for display — format_pvalue
text: Vectorised helper format numeric p-values readable strings.

title: Format p-values for display — format_pvalue
code: format_pvalue(x, p_symbol = "p", include_p_symbol = TRUE)

title: Format p-values for display — format_pvalue
text: x Numeric vector p-values p_symbol Character; symbol prefix p-values (default 'p') include_p_symbol Logical; whether include p_symbol prefix (default TRUE)

title: Format p-values for display — format_pvalue
text: Character vector formatted p-values, optionally prefixed p_symbol include_p_symbol TRUE.

title: Format p-values for display — format_pvalue

what: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: Create stacked percent bar chart x categorical variable broken second categorical variable. plot includes within-group percentages, group sample sizes bar, (optionally) subtitle showing Cramer's V test p-value.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
code: plot_2_categorical_vars(   d,   xvar,   yvar,   xvar_label = NULL,   yvar_label = NULL,   yvar_colors = NULL,   title = NULL,   title_nchar_wrap = 30,   show_effect_size = TRUE,   n_pct_size = 3.5,   pct_digits = 0,   plot_horizontal = FALSE,   flip = FALSE,   xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap = 20,   y_max = 110,   n_ypos = y_max - 5 )

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: d data.frame containing variables. xvar Character scalar, name categorical x variable (column d). yvar Character scalar, name categorical y variable (column d). xvar_label Optional character scalar use x axis label. yvar_label Optional character scalar use legend label. yvar_colors Optional character vector colours use y-variable levels. Must length equal nlevels(d[[yvar]]). Provide colour names (e.g. "red") hex codes (e.g. "#FF0000"). NULL (default) ggplot2's default palette used. title Optional character scalar plot title. title_nchar_wrap Integer scalar. Maximum number characters per line title. show_effect_size Logical; TRUE subtitle include Cramer's V p-value. n_pct_size Numeric scalar. Point size used percent labels inside stacked bars group N labels bars. Must single positive numeric value. pct_digits Integer scalar (default 0). Number decimal places show within-group percent labels (e.g. 0 => "12%", 1 => "12.3%"). Must single non-negative numeric value. plot_horizontal Logical scalar. Deprecated/alias flip. TRUE plot shown horizontal bars. Prefer using flip. flip Logical scalar (default FALSE). TRUE plot flipped show horizontal bars. xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap Integer scalar. Maximum number characters per line x-axis group labels. Longer labels wrapped multiple lines. y_max Numeric scalar. Maximum y-axis limit plot. n_ypos Numeric scalar. y-axis position group N labels.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: list ggplot2 object, Cramer's V effect size, p-value.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
code: data(mtcars) mtcars$cyl <- factor(mtcars$cyl) mtcars$gear <- factor(mtcars$gear) p <- plot_2_categorical_vars(   d = mtcars,   xvar = "cyl",   yvar = "gear",   xvar_label = 'Cylinders',   yvar_label = 'Gears' ) p$ggplot

title: Bar plot by group — plot_bars
text: Create grouped bar plot (geom_col) data frame. Reorders factor levels xvar yvar (descending) maps fill xvar. Accepts named unnamed color vector; unnamed vectors must match number groups.

title: Bar plot by group — plot_bars
code: plot_bars(   dat,   yvar,   xvar,   colors = NULL,   sort = c("desc", "asc", "none"),   title = NULL,   xvar_label = NULL,   yvar_label = NULL,   show_legend = FALSE )

title: Bar plot by group — plot_bars
text: dat data.frame containing data plot. yvar Character. Name numeric outcome column. xvar Character. Name grouping column (coerced/reordered factor). colors Optional character vector fill colours. named, names match group levels. unnamed, length must equal number groups. Missing group names filled "black". sort Character. sort bars: "desc" descending, "asc" ascending, "none" reordering. title Optional plot title xvar_label Optional character label x variable. NULL label obtained using labelled::get_variable_labels(). yvar_label Optional character label y variable. NULL label obtained using labelled::get_variable_labels(). show_legend Logical. Whether show legend fill colors. Default FALSE.

title: Bar plot by group — plot_bars
code: dat <- data.frame(group = c("A", "B", "C"), score = c(5, 3, 8)) plot_bars(dat, yvar = "score", xvar = "group", sort = "desc")

what: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
text: Create horizontal dot--whisker plot showing point estimates confidence intervals labeled rows. Optionally offset points use different shapes colors grouping column supplied. Optionally append p-value barplot right via patchwork.

title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
code: plot_dot_whiskers(   data,   group_col = NULL,   x = "est",   xmin = "conf.low",   xmax = "conf.high",   label_col = "cell_line",   dodge_width = 0.3,   style = c("color", "shape"),   sep_linetype = "solid",   sep_linewidth = 0.4,   sep_color = "black",   vline_xintercept = 0,   vline_linetype = "dashed",   vline_color = "black",   point_shapes = c(21, 24, 22, 25, 23),   pvalue_col = NULL,   pvalue_plot_width = 0.3,   ... )

title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
text: data data.frame tibble containing columns referenced x, xmin, xmax, label_col. group_col Optional string name factor grouping column. provided, points offset group visibility. Factor levels control ordering direction dodge offset: first level plotted top row. x String name estimate column. Default "est". xmin String name lower-interval column. Default "conf.low". xmax String name upper-interval column. Default "conf.high". label_col String name label/row identifier column. Must factor; factor levels control top--bottom row ordering (first level appears top). Default "cell_line". dodge_width Numeric. Total vertical spread across groups. Default 0.3. style String. Controls group encoding group_col supplied. "color" (default) uses different colors per group draws horizontal separator lines rows. "shape" uses color per label_col row different point shapes per group. sep_linetype Line type row separator lines style = "color". Default "solid". sep_linewidth Line width row separator lines style = "color". Default 0.4. sep_color Color row separator lines style = "color". Default "black". vline_xintercept Numeric. Position vertical reference line. Default 0. vline_linetype Line type vertical reference line. Default "dashed". vline_color Color vertical reference line. Default "black". point_shapes Integer vector point shapes use style = "shape". Must least many elements levels group_col. Defaults c(21, 24, 22, 25, 23) (5 groups). See graphics::points() shape codes. pvalue_col Optional string name column data containing one p-value per label_col row. supplied, plot_pvalue_barplot() appended right using patchwork. p-value used row taken first occurrence within label_col group, column must constant within row. Requires patchwork package. pvalue_plot_width Relative width p-value panel passed patchwork::wrap_plots() widths. Default 0.3. ... Additional arguments passed plot_pvalue_barplot() pvalue_col supplied.

title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
text: ggplot2 object, patchwork object pvalue_col supplied.

title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_dot_whiskers
code: df <- data.frame(   cell_line = factor(c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C"), levels = c('A', 'B', 'C')),   est       = c(0.2, 0.35, -0.1, 0.05, 0.5, 0.3),   conf.low  = c(0.0, 0.10, -0.3, -0.10, 0.2, 0.1),   conf.high = c(0.4, 0.60,  0.1,  0.20, 0.8, 0.5),   group     = factor(c("g1", "g2", "g1", "g2", "g1", "g2"), levels = c("g1", "g2")),   pvalue    = c(0.01, 0.01, 0.4, 0.4, 0.001, 0.001) )  # color style: groups distinguished by color, separator lines between rows plot_dot_whiskers(df, group_col = "group", style = "color")   # shape style: groups distinguished by point shape, rows colored by label plot_dot_whiskers(df, group_col = "group", style = "shape")   # with p-value barplot appended on the right plot_dot_whiskers(   df,   group_col  = "group",   style      = "color",   pvalue_col = "pvalue",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   # no grouping, with p-value barplot appended on the right df_single <- data.frame(   cell_line = factor(c("A", "B", "C"), levels = c("A", "B", "C")),   est       = c(0.2, -0.1, 0.5),   conf.low  = c(0.0, -0.3, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.4,  0.1, 0.8),   pvalue    = c(0.01, 0.4, 0.001) )  plot_dot_whiskers(   df_single,   pvalue_col = "pvalue",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

what: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
text: combined tile + point "dotmap" visualizes effect (size direction) p-value (tile fill). function returns ggplot object patchwork composition combined p-value barplot requested.

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
code: plot_dotmap(   data,   x,   y,   effect,   p,   dot_size_vals = c(-2, -1, -0.5, -0.25, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2),   dot_size_labels = as.character(dot_size_vals),   dot_range = c(5, 30),   palette = c(positive = "darkorange1", negative = "dodgerblue2"),   xlab_angle = 0,   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   fill_limits = NULL,   legend_pvalue_title = NULL,   legend_dotsize_title = expression(bold("Effect size")),   add_combined_pvalue_barplot = FALSE,   combine_pvalue_method = c("CMC", "fisher", "MCM", "cauchy", "minp_bonferroni"),   sort_by_pvalue = TRUE,   only_show_top_sig = NULL,   ...,   patchwork_widths = c(3, 1) )

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
text: data data.frame tibble containing plotting variables x Character; name variable data use x-axis/columns y Character; name variable data use y-axis/rows effect Character; column name numeric variable data use dot size color (direction) p Character; column name numeric variable data use tile fill (p-value) dot_size_vals Numeric vector reference effect values used size legend (signed indicate direction) dot_size_labels Character vector labels size legend; must length dot_size_vals dot_range Numeric(2) range point sizes (min, max) palette Named character vector elements "positive" "negative" specifying dot fill colours positive/negative effects xlab_angle Numeric angle rotate x-axis labels (degrees) mlog10_transform_pvalue Logical; TRUE fill uses -log10(p) instead raw p fill_limits Numeric(2) NULL; limits fill scale (c(min, max)). NULL sensible default used (c(0,3) -log10(p) c(0,1) raw p) legend_pvalue_title Character expression NULL; override title p-value (tile fill) legend. NULL automatic title used. legend_dotsize_title Character expression; title dot-size legend add_combined_pvalue_barplot Logical; TRUE adds combined p-value barplot right dotmap (requires patchwork) combine_pvalue_method Character; method combining p-values barplot. One : "CMC", "fisher", "MCM", "cauchy", "minp_bonferroni". Defaults "CMC". sort_by_pvalue Logical; TRUE (default) rows (levels y) sorted combined p-value (ascending). Requires p-values present per group. only_show_top_sig Numeric(1) NULL; adding combined p-value barplot, positive integer top X significant rows combined p-value shown (default NULL, show ) ... Additional arguments passed plot_pvalue_barplot() add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE patchwork_widths Numeric(2); widths passed patchwork::wrap_plots() adding combined p-value barplot (default c(3, 1))

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
text: ggplot2::ggplot object add_combined_pvalue_barplot = FALSE, patchwork composition object (patchwork) add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE.

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
text: tile fill encodes p-values (optionally transformed -log10(p)), overplotted points encode effect size (size) direction (fill color). NA values effect marked "×" symbol. combined p-value barplot requested function groups y computes combined p-value using combine_pvalues(); combined panel aligned vertically main dotmap.

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
code: set.seed(42) genes <- paste0("gene", 1:6) df <- expand.grid(col = c("A", "B", "C"), row = genes, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) df$effect <- rnorm(nrow(df), mean = 0, sd = 1.2)         # realistic effect sizes df$mlog10_p <- runif(nrow(df), min = 0, max = 3)         # -log10(p) between 0 and 3 df$p <- 10^(-df$mlog10_p) df$row <- factor(df$row, levels = rev(genes)) plot_dotmap(df, x = "col", y = "row", effect = "effect", p = "p",             mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values.  # Add Fisher's combination pvalue barplot on the right which combines p-values across columns for each row category plot_dotmap(   df,   x = "col",   y = "row",   effect = "effect",   p = "p",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE,   combine_pvalue_method = "CMC"   ) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values.

what: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
title: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
text: Create horizontal interval plot effect-size estimates (values 0 1) lower/upper confidence bounds. y-axis shows effect labels x-axis gives effect size values.

title: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
code: plot_effectsize_01_intervals(   d,   variable_name,   value_name,   group_name = NULL,   group_label = group_name,   colors = NULL,   xaxis_label = "Effect size",   lower_name = "lower",   upper_name = "upper",   title = "Effect size estimates with 95% confidence intervals",   sort = TRUE,   descending = FALSE,   dot_size = 3 )

title: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
text: d data.frame: data containing effect labels, estimates bounds. variable_name character(1): column name d variable names y-axis. value_name character(1): column name d estimates (numeric, expected 0..1). group_name character(1) NULL: optional column name d used color points/lines group. group_label character(1) NULL: label show legend group_name used. Defaults group_name. colors character(1) NULL: optional character vector (named character vector) colours map groups; names match group values. NULL (default) uses ggplot2 defaults. xaxis_label character(1): label x axis (default "Effect size"). lower_name character(1): column name d CI lower bound (numeric, default "lower"). upper_name character(1): column name d CI upper bound (numeric, default "upper"). title character(1): plot title (default "Effect size estimates 95% confidence intervals"). sort logical: sort rows estimate plotting (default TRUE). descending logical: TRUE place largest estimates top (default TRUE). dot_size numeric(1): size points (default 3). Use smaller values make points less prominent relative CI lines.

title: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
text: ggplot object (horizontal interval plot).

title: Plot effect-size estimates and confidence intervals. All values must be between 0 and 1. — plot_effectsize_01_intervals
code: df <- data.frame(   effect = paste0("Effect ", LETTERS[1:5]),   estimate = c(0.12, 0.35, 0.58, 0.74, 0.91),   lower = c(0.05, 0.22, 0.45, 0.60, 0.82),   upper = c(0.20, 0.47, 0.69, 0.85, 0.97),   group = rep(c("One","Two"), length.out = 5) )  # Example with no group colors plot_effectsize_01_intervals(   d = df,   variable_name = "effect",   value_name = "estimate",   lower_name = "lower",   upper_name = "upper" )   # Example with group colors plot_effectsize_01_intervals(   d = df,   variable_name = "effect",   value_name = "estimate",   group_name = "group",   group_label = "My groups",   lower_name = "lower",   upper_name = "upper",   dot_size = 1.5 )

what: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
title: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
text: Converts tidy (long-format) data frame matrix renders Heatmap optional row/column annotations, splits, row scaling.

title: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
code: plot_heatmap(   df,   row_var,   col_var,   value_var,   row_covariates = NULL,   col_covariates = NULL,   row_split_var = NULL,   col_split_var = NULL,   heatmap_colors = NULL,   anno_colors = NULL,   scale_rows = FALSE,   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = TRUE,   show_row_names = TRUE,   row_names_side = "left",   show_column_names = TRUE,   return_details = FALSE,   heatmap_legend_title = "Value",   rect_gp = grid::gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1),   ... )

title: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
text: df data frame long (tidy) format. row_var <data-masking> Column df used row identifiers. col_var <data-masking> Column df used column identifiers. value_var <data-masking> Numeric column df used heatmap fill values. row_covariates Character vector column names df use row annotations. Default NULL. col_covariates Character vector column names df use column annotations. Default NULL. row_split_var Character. Column name df used split rows. Must \(\geq 2\) levels. Default NULL. col_split_var Character. Column name df used split columns. Must \(\geq 2\) levels. Default NULL. heatmap_colors color mapping passed Heatmap's col argument. Default NULL, case numeric color mapping automatically generated (e.g. via colorRamp2) rather using ComplexHeatmap's built-default. anno_colors Named list specifying colors annotation covariates. discrete (categorical) covariates, element named character vector mapping annotation levels hex colors; levels supplied auto-colored. continuous (numeric) covariates, element may instead function maps numeric values colors (e.g., function created circlize::colorRamp2), applied numeric annotation values generate colors. Default NULL. scale_rows Logical. Whether z-score scale rows. cluster_rows Logical. Whether cluster rows. Default TRUE. cluster_columns Logical. Whether cluster columns. Default TRUE. show_row_names Logical. Default TRUE. row_names_side "left" "right". Default "left". show_column_names Logical. Default TRUE. return_details Logical. TRUE, returns list drawn heatmap object, final annotation colors, levels. Default FALSE. heatmap_legend_title Character. Title heatmap legend rect_gp grid::gpar object graphical parameters heatmap cells (passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap's rect_gp argument). Use control cell borders lines (e.g. col, lwd, lty) fill behavior. Default: grid::gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1). hide borders use rect_gp = grid::gpar(col = NA). ... Additional arguments passed Heatmap.

title: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
text: Invisibly returns drawn HeatmapList object, named list elements ht, final_colors, levels return_details = TRUE.

title: Plot a heatmap from tidy data — plot_heatmap
code: data(ex_data_heatmap)  ht_cols_small <- circlize::colorRamp2(     c(min(1:16), mean(1:16), max(1:16)),     c("#145afc", "white", "#ee4445") )  ann_cols_small <- list(     group = c(G1 = "#1b9e77", G2 = "#d95f02"),     direction = c(up = "#e41a1c", down = "#4daf4a"),     is_immune_gene = c(yes = "#fb8072", no = "#d9d9d9"),     sample_type = c(input = "#8dd3c7", IP = "#80b1d3"),     condition = c(healthy = "#b3de69", EAE = "#fccde5") )  # default colors plot_heatmap(     df = ex_data_heatmap,     row_var = external_gene_name,     col_var = sample,     value_var = expression,     row_covariates = c("is_immune_gene", "direction"),     col_covariates = c("sample_type", "condition", "group"),     row_split_var = "direction", # up vs down (2 slices)     col_split_var = "group", # G1 vs G2 (2 slices)     scale_rows = FALSE,     cluster_rows = TRUE,     cluster_columns = TRUE,     return_details = TRUE,     row_names_side = "left" )  # custom colors plot_heatmap(     df = ex_data_heatmap,     row_var = external_gene_name,     col_var = sample,     value_var = expression,     row_covariates = c("is_immune_gene", "direction"),     col_covariates = c("sample_type", "condition", "group"),     row_split_var = "direction",     col_split_var = "group",     scale_rows = FALSE,     cluster_rows = FALSE,     cluster_columns = FALSE,     heatmap_colors = ht_cols_small,     anno_colors = ann_cols_small,     return_details = TRUE )  ### Continuous row and column covariates via colorRamp2 functions rng_log2fc <- range(ex_data_heatmap$log2fc, na.rm = TRUE) col_fun_log2fc <- circlize::colorRamp2(     c(rng_log2fc[1], 0, rng_log2fc[2]),     c("#762a83", "white", "#e66101") )  rng_qc <- range(ex_data_heatmap$qc_score, na.rm = TRUE) col_fun_qc <- circlize::colorRamp2(     c(rng_qc[1], mean(rng_qc), rng_qc[2]),     c("#ffffcc", "#41b6c4", "#0c2c84") )  expr_rng <- range(ex_data_heatmap$expression, na.rm = TRUE) expr_cols <- circlize::colorRamp2(     c(expr_rng[1], mean(expr_rng), expr_rng[2]),     c("#313695", "#f7f7f7", "#a50026") )  plot_heatmap(     df = ex_data_heatmap,     row_var = external_gene_name,     col_var = sample,     value_var = expression,     row_covariates = c("log2fc", "is_immune_gene", "direction"),     col_covariates = c("qc_score", "condition", "sample_type"),     col_split_var = "group",     row_split_var = "direction",     heatmap_colors = expr_cols,     anno_colors = list(         log2fc         = col_fun_log2fc,         qc_score       = col_fun_qc,         is_immune_gene = c(yes = "#e7298a", no = "#a6761d"),         direction      = c(up = "#1f78b4", down = "#e31a1c"),         condition      = c(healthy = "#1b9e77", EAE = "#d95f02"),         sample_type    = c(input = "#7570b3", IP = "#66a61e")     ),     scale_rows = FALSE,     cluster_rows = TRUE,     cluster_columns = TRUE,     show_row_names = TRUE,     row_names_side = "left",     heatmap_legend_title = "Expression" )  ##### make a categorical heatmap cat_data <- ex_data_heatmap cat_data$expression <- as.character(cut(     cat_data$expression,     breaks = c(-Inf, 3, 6, Inf),     labels = c("low", "medium", "high") ))   # default colors plot_heatmap(     df = cat_data,     row_var = external_gene_name,     col_var = sample,     value_var = expression,     row_covariates = c("is_immune_gene", "direction"),     col_covariates = c("sample_type", "condition", "group"),     row_split_var = "direction", # up vs down (2 slices)     col_split_var = "group", # G1 vs G2 (2 slices)     scale_rows = FALSE,     cluster_rows = TRUE,     cluster_columns = TRUE,     return_details = TRUE,     row_names_side = "left" )   # custom colors and clustering heat_cat_colors <- c(     low = "#313695",     medium = "#f7f7f7",     high = "#a50026" )   clust_dist_gower <- function(x) {    # x is the matrix supplied by Heatmap; convert to factors if needed    df <- as.data.frame(x)    df[] <- lapply(df, factor)    stats::as.dist(cluster::daisy(df, metric = "gower")) }  plot_heatmap(     df = cat_data,     row_var = external_gene_name,     col_var = sample,     heatmap_colors = heat_cat_colors,     value_var = expression,     row_covariates = c("is_immune_gene", "direction"),     col_covariates = c("sample_type", "condition", "group"),     row_split_var = "direction", # up vs down (2 slices)     col_split_var = "group", # G1 vs G2 (2 slices)     scale_rows = FALSE,     cluster_rows = TRUE,     cluster_columns = TRUE,     return_details = TRUE,     row_names_side = "left",     clustering_distance_rows = clust_dist_gower,     clustering_distance_columns = clust_dist_gower )

what: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
title: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
text: Create violin plot overlaid narrow boxplot two groups run Wilcoxon rank-sum test. Returns ggplot object tidy Wilcoxon result.

title: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
code: plot_numeric_by_2groups(   yvar,   group,   d,   title = NULL,   colors = c("white", "white"),   digits = 1 )

title: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
text: yvar character(1) Name numeric outcome column d. group character(1) Name factor column d (must 2 levels). d data.frame Data containing yvar group. title character Optional plot title. NULL, default generated. colors character vector Length-2 vector fill colours (default c('white', 'white')); NULL default ggplot2 fill scale used.  supplied, must character vector length equal number groups (two filtering); names, must exactly match factor levels. digits numeric(1) Number decimal places use subtitle statistics.

title: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
text: list elements: ggplot ggplot2 object (violin + boxplot). wilcox tibble Wilcoxon test results (broom::tidy).

title: Violin + boxplot with Wilcoxon rank-sum test — plot_numeric_by_2groups
code: mtcars$am <- factor(mtcars$am) res <- plot_numeric_by_2groups("mpg", "am", mtcars) #> Warning: cannot compute exact p-value with ties #> Warning: cannot compute exact confidence intervals with ties res$ggplot  res$wilcox #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   estimate statistic p.value conf.low conf.high method       alternative outcome #>      <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>        <chr>       <chr>   #> 1    -6.80        42 0.00187    -11.7     -2.90 Wilcoxon ra… two.sided   mpg

what: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
title: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
text: Create violin plot overlaid boxplot showing distribution numeric variable split factor. function expects grouping column factor outcome numeric.

title: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
code: plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups(   yvar,   group,   d,   yvar_label = NULL,   group_label = NULL,   title = NULL,   group_colors = NULL,   show_effect_size = TRUE,   n_boot = 1000,   xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap = 20,   pairwise_wilcoxon_test_letters = TRUE )

title: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
text: yvar Character scalar. Column name (d) numeric variable plot. Must length 1 refer numeric column d. group Character scalar. Column name (d) grouping factor. Must length 1 refer factor column d. d data.frame containing variables. yvar_label Optional character scalar y-axis label. Defaults yvar. supplied, must length 1. group_label Optional character scalar x-axis label. Defaults group. supplied, must length 1. title Optional character scalar plot title. Defaults "<yvar> <group>". supplied, must length 1. group_colors Optional character vector colours use grouping levels. Must length equal nlevels(d[[group]]) supplied. Use colour names hex codes. show_effect_size Logical scalar. TRUE subtitle include epsilon-squared p-value. n_boot Integer-like numeric scalar. Number bootstrap samples estimating CI epsilon-squared. Default 1000. Must single numeric value >= 10. xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap Numeric scalar. Maximum line width (number characters) used wrap group labels x-axis appending sample size. Defaults 20. Must single positive numeric value. pairwise_wilcoxon_test_letters Logical scalar. TRUE (default), run pairwise Wilcoxon tests, compute compact-letter group labels display group. FALSE, skip tests show letters.

title: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
text: list components: ggplot ggplot2 object produced function. stats data frame effect size results (epsilon-squared estimate, CI, p-value, sample size, etc.). letters named character vector compact-letter grouping codes generated pairwise Wilcoxon tests, NULL pairwise_wilcoxon_test_letters FALSE.

title: Violin + boxplot of a numeric variable by a grouping factor — plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups
code: data(mtcars) mtcars$gear <- factor(mtcars$gear) p <- plot_numeric_by_3plusgroups(   yvar = "mpg",   group = "gear",   d = mtcars ) p$ggplot

title: Plot p-value barplot — plot_pvalue_barplot
text: Create horizontal barplot p-values (optionally -log10 transformed) optional significance vertical line optional fill mapping.

title: Plot p-value barplot — plot_pvalue_barplot
code: plot_pvalue_barplot(   data,   x,   y,   fill = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   width = 0.6,   xlim = NULL,   xbreaks = NULL,   xlab = "p-value",   vline = TRUE,   vline_linetype = "dashed",   vline_color = "red",   show_y_labels = FALSE,   mlog10_transform_pvalue = FALSE,   also_show_qvalue = TRUE,   custom_qvalues = NULL,   color_qvalue = "grey",   color_pvalue = "black" )

title: Plot p-value barplot — plot_pvalue_barplot
text: data data.frame tibble containing variables. x Character, name column raw p-values y Character, name column y-axis categories (factor character). fill Character NULL, column name use fill; NULL draw solid black bars. Ignored also_show_qvalue = TRUE. alpha Numeric significance threshold vertical line (default 0.05). mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE vertical line drawn -log10(alpha). width Numeric bar width (passed geom_col). xlim Numeric vector length 2 giving x-axis limits; computed NULL. xbreaks Numeric vector x-axis breaks; computed NULL. xlab Character, x-axis label. vline Logical, whether draw vertical line alpha (-log10(alpha)). vline_linetype Character, linetype vertical line. vline_color Character, color vertical line. show_y_labels Logical, whether show y-axis labels (default FALSE). mlog10_transform_pvalue Logical; TRUE compute -log10(p) plotting/order format x-axis tick labels p-values (10^-x). also_show_qvalue Logical; TRUE compute FDR-adjusted q-values (Benjamini-Hochberg) (use custom_qvalues supplied) draw two overlapping bars per row: p q shown. Note drawing order: mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE p-value bar drawn first (behind) q-value bar drawn top. mlog10_transform_pvalue = FALSE q-value bar drawn first (behind) p-value bar drawn top. TRUE, 'fill' argument ignored fixed colors used p/q bars. custom_qvalues Character NULL; column name data containing user-supplied q-values. supplied also_show_qvalue = TRUE, values used instead computing FDR-adjusted q-values. color_qvalue Character, color q-value bars also_show_qvalue = TRUE. color_pvalue Character, color p-value bars also_show_qvalue = TRUE.

title: Plot p-value barplot — plot_pvalue_barplot
text: ggplot2 object (invisible plot object returned).

title: Plot p-value barplot — plot_pvalue_barplot
code: set.seed(123) n <- 4 example_df <- tibble::tibble(   cell_line = paste0("Cell", sprintf("%02d", 1:n)),   pvalue = 10^(-runif(n, 0.2, 2.8)),   group = rep(c("A", "B"), length.out = n) ) example_df$cell_line <- factor(   example_df$cell_line,   levels = rev(example_df$cell_line) ) plot_pvalue_barplot(   data = example_df,   x = "pvalue",   y = "cell_line",   fill = NULL,   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   show_y_labels = TRUE,   also_show_qvalue = TRUE )

what: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
title: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
text: Plot Kaplan–Meier curve (confidence intervals risk table) using supplied Surv object. grouping variable exactly two levels function fits Cox model annotates hazard ratio (95% CI) p-value; two groups displays log‑rank p-value.

title: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
code: plot_survival_curves(   surv_obj,   data,   group_var = "met_exercise_guidelines",   time_limits = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   annotate_y = 0.99,   annotate_x = NULL,   x_label = "Time (units??)",   y_label = NULL,   title = NULL,   custom_hr_pvalue_text = NULL,   type = c("survival", "risk") )

title: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
text: surv_obj Surv object (can right‑censored left‑truncated). data data frame containing variables referenced surv_obj group_var. group_var Character, name grouping column data. time_limits Numeric(2), x-axis limits plot (default c(50, 100)). x_breaks Numeric, breaks x axis. annotate_y Numeric, y position annotation text (default 0.99). annotate_x Numeric NULL, x position annotation; NULL uses rightmost time_limits. x_label Character, label x axis (default \"Time\"). y_label Character, label y axis (default \"Probability Event-free\"). title Character NULL, plot title (default NULL). custom_hr_pvalue_text Character NULL; provided, string used annotation instead automatically computed HR/p-value (two groups) log‑rank p-value (>2 groups). type Character, one "survival" "risk"; passed ggsurvfit(). "survival" default y_label "Probability Event-Free"; "risk" default y_label "Probability Event".

title: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
text: ggplot object (ggsurvfit).

title: Kaplan–Meier plot from a Surv object — plot_survival_curves
code: data(cancer, package = "survival")  # Example with two groups lung$sex <- factor(lung$sex, labels = c("Male", "Female")) surv_obj <- with(lung, survival::Surv(time, status == 2)) plot_survival_curves(surv_obj, lung, group_var = "sex")   # Example with more than two groups lung$ph.ecog[lung$ph.ecog == 3] <- NA lung$ph.ecog <- factor(lung$ph.ecog) surv_obj <- with(lung, survival::Surv(time, status == 2)) plot_survival_curves(surv_obj, lung, group_var = "ph.ecog")   # Cumulative incidence plot plot_survival_curves(surv_obj, lung, group_var = "ph.ecog")

what: Build an upset plot from a named list of sets — plot_upset

code: plot_upset(   list_input,   xlab = "Combination",   ylab = "Count",   title = "",   n_intersections = 10,   ymax_offset = 0.5,   order_by = c("freq", "degree"),   backend = c("UpSetR", "ggupset"),   text_scale = 1.5 )

Default: 20 ymax_offset Numeric offset added computed ymax provide headroom tallest bar. Default: 0.5 order_by order intersections: "freq" "degree". Default: "freq" backend backend use plotting: 'UpSetR' 'ggupset' text_scale Numeric scaling factor text size (used backend 'UpSetR')

text: ggplot object

title: Build an upset plot from a named list of sets — plot_upset
code: list_input <- list(   A = c(1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11, 12, 13),   B = c(1, 2, 4, 5, 10),   C = c(1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13) ) plot_upset(list_input) #> Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0. #> ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`. #> ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package. #>   Please report the issue to the authors. #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package. #>   Please report the issue to the authors. #> Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package. #>   Please report the issue to the authors.

what: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
title: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
text: Print element list level-2 heading (##) suitable Quarto tabsets. title NULL list names used. Typical use printing ggplot objects printable plot objects becomes separate tab.

title: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
code: quarto_html_tabset_list(list_obj, title = NULL)

title: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
text: list_obj list printable R objects (e.g. ggplot objects). title Optional character vector titles use element; NULL names(list_obj) used.

title: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
text: input list, returned invisibly.

title: Render a named list as a Quarto tabset — quarto_html_tabset_list
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) plots <- list(   One = ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, wt)) + geom_point(),   Two = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, qsec)) + geom_point() ) quarto_tabset_list(plots) } # }

what: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
title: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
text: Convenience wrapper builds gtsummary::tbl_summary overall dataset.

title: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
code: table_overall(d)

title: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
text: d data.frame Dataset containing variables summarize.

title: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
text: gtsummary::tbl_summary object overall cohort.

title: Create a table that summarizes the entire cohort. — table_overall
code: df <- data.frame(age = rnorm(20, 50, 10), sex = sample(c("M","F"), 20, TRUE)) table_overall(df) #> Setting theme "language: en" #> Setting theme "Compact"     Characteristic       OverallN = 20     age     Median (Q1, Q3) 51.2 (44.9, 56.0)    Mean +/- SD 51.2 +/- 10.0    Min, Max 30.3, 67.9sex     F 7 (35%)    M 13 (65%)

what: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
text: Overall column reports percentages overall cohort. group columns report row-wise percentages.

title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
code: table_overall_and_group(d, name_groupvar, group_header = "**Group**")

title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
text: d data.frame. Dataset containing variables summarize. name_groupvar character(1). Name grouping variable (column name) d used produce grouped summary. group_header character(1). Label used tab spanner grouped columns merged table.

title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
text: gtsummary table object

title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
text: function builds two tbl_summary objects: one overall dataset (grouping column removed) one stratified specified grouping variable. merges single table custom tab spanner grouped columns.

title: Create overall and by-group summary table — table_overall_and_group
code: df <- data.frame(   grp = rep(c("A","B"), each = 10),   age = rnorm(20, 50, 10),   sex = sample(c("M","F"), 20, TRUE) ) table_overall_and_group(df, "grp") #> Setting theme "language: en" #> Setting theme "Compact"     Characteristic       OverallN = 20                Group            An = 10       Bn = 10     age       Median (Q1, Q3) 45.7 (42.3, 50.8) 42.9 (42.2, 47.2) 48.8 (43.7, 52.1)    Min, Max 35.7, 56.6 35.7, 56.2 41.0, 56.6sex       F 11 (55%) 4 (36%) 7 (64%)    M 9 (45%) 6 (67%) 3 (33%)

text: plot_dot_whiskers plotting effect sizes confidence intervals, optionally group pvalue barplot default, white gridlines around cells Add support categorical heatmaps Support clustering categorical data quarto_html_tabset_list: convert named list object tabset knitting HTML format_pvalue: function format pvalues display plots Updated methods contemporary best practices default now shows pvalues qvalues stacked Changed default combo method CMC recommended literature option show q-values combined pvalue barplot 5 methods supported combining pvalues show x symbol dot missing
